content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices numbered 1 through NNN as a sequence of N−1N-1N−1 edges — pairs (a1,b1),(a2,b2),…,(aN−1,bN−1)(a_1, b_1), (a_2, b_2), \ldots, (a_{N-1}, b_{N-1})(a1​,b1​),(a2​,b2​),…,(aN−1​,bN−1​). Let's define the value of a sequence AAA with length N−1N-1N−1 as
F(A)=∑i=1N−1Ai⋅Wi ,F(A)=\sum_{i=1}^{N-1} A_i \cdot W_i \,,F(A)=∑i=1N−1​Ai​⋅Wi​,
where W1,W2,…,WN−1W_1, W_2, \ldots, W_{N-1}W1​,W2​,…,WN−1​ are given weights.

You may change the order of the pairs and the order of elements within any pairs. Formally, consider a reordering (P,R)(P, R)(P,R) — any permutation P=(P1,P2,…,PN−1)P = (P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_{N-1})P=(P1​,P2​,…,PN−1​) of the integers 111 through N−1N-1N−1 and any subset RRR of these integers (possibly containing all of them). Let's define two sequences C=(cP1,cP2,…,cPN−1)C = (c_{P_1}, c_{P_2}, \ldots, c_{P_{N-1}})C=(cP1​​,cP2​​,…,cPN−1​​) and D=(dP1,dP2,…,dPN−1)D = (d_{P_1}, d_{P_2}, \ldots, d_{P_{N-1}})D=(dP1​​,dP2​​,…,dPN−1​​), where for each valid iii, ci=aic_i = a_ici​=ai​ and di=bid_i = b_idi​=bi​ if i∉Ri \not\in Ri∈R, or ci=bic_i = b_ici​=bi​ and di=aid_i = a_idi​=ai​ if i∈Ri \in Ri∈R. If DDD is **strictly** increasing, the reordering is valid and its value is F(C)F(C)F(C).

Find the maximum value of a valid reordering, i.e. the maximum F(C)F(C)F(C), and the number of valid reorderings that have this maximum value.
constraints: 1≤ai,bi≤N1 \le a_i, b_i \le N1≤ai​,bi​≤N for each valid iii
pairs (ai,bi)(a_i, b_i)(ai​,bi​) are edges of a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers W1,W2,…,WN−1W_1, W_2, \ldots, W_{N-1}W1​,W2​,…,WN−1​.
N−1N-1N−1 lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers aia_iai​ and bib_ibi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers — the maximum value of a reordering and the number of reorderings with the maximum value.
